because part of the energy goes into the recoil of nucleus ￼.
If the excited nucleus ￼ decays at rest, the emitted photon has an energy smaller than the level spacing
Resonant absorption as a direction filter
Therefore, such a photon cannot be resonantly absorbed by another nucleus B at rest: it is simply not energetic enough.
